help.body_cfind:
Query a remote PACS for patients and studies using the DICOM Query/Retrieve service.

─── Buttons ───────────────────────────────────────────────
C-ECHO (Ping)      Verifies the remote AE is reachable and responding.
                   Always run this first to confirm connectivity before a C-FIND.
Run C-FIND         Sends a query using any combination of the filter fields.
                   Leave all fields blank to retrieve everything (use with care).
C-MOVE             Instructs the remote PACS to push the selected study to the
                   Destination AE you specify. That AE must have a Storage SCP
                   running and be known to the source PACS.

─── Query Level ────────────────────────────────────────────
The Query Level determines how granular the results are:

  PATIENT  Returns one row per patient. Works only with the Patient Root model.
           Useful for patient-level lookups (e.g. find all studies for a patient).

  STUDY    Returns one row per study. The most commonly used level.
           Works with both Study Root and Patient Root models.

  SERIES   Returns one row per series within a study.
           Requires a Study Instance UID to narrow results.

  IMAGE    Returns one row per SOP instance (individual DICOM file).
           Requires a Series Instance UID. Use sparingly — very verbose.

─── Model (Information Model) ──────────────────────────────
The Information Model defines the DICOM service class used for the query.
Most PACS systems support both, but not all do.

  STUDY  (Study Root Q/R — recommended for most use cases)
         Hierarchy: STUDY → SERIES → IMAGE
         Valid levels: STUDY, SERIES, IMAGE
         ✗  PATIENT level is NOT supported in this model.
         Use this when you already have an Accession, Date, or Study UID.

  PATIENT  (Patient Root Q/R)
           Hierarchy: PATIENT → STUDY → SERIES → IMAGE
           Valid levels: PATIENT, STUDY, SERIES, IMAGE
           Use this when searching by Patient ID or Patient Name without
           a specific study context, or when the PACS requires it.

─── Valid Combinations ─────────────────────────────────────
  Model     │  Supported Query Levels
  ──────────┼───────────────────────────────────────
  STUDY     │  STUDY  ·  SERIES  ·  IMAGE
  PATIENT   │  PATIENT  ·  STUDY  ·  SERIES  ·  IMAGE

The tool will warn you and auto-correct incompatible combinations.

─── Date Format ────────────────────────────────────────────
Study Date accepts DICOM date format: YYYYMMDD or a range YYYYMMDD-YYYYMMDD
Example: 20240101-20241231 returns all studies from 2024.

─── Tips ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
• Patient Name supports wildcards: SMITH* or *SMITH* (DICOM wildcard is *)
• Double-click any result row to inspect all returned DICOM tags.
• If C-FIND returns 0 results, try relaxing filters or check the Query Level.
help.body_cstore:
Send DICOM files from disk to a remote Storage SCP using the C-STORE service.

─── Workflow ───────────────────────────────────────────────
1. Set the destination AE Title, Host, and Port.
2. Add Files — pick individual .dcm files, or
   Add Folder — recursively adds all files in a directory tree.
3. Review the queued file list.
4. Click Send All (C-STORE) to transfer.

─── Notes ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
• Each file is sent as a separate C-STORE request within the same association.
• The SCP must accept the SOP Class of each file (e.g. CT Image Storage).
• Transfer Syntax negotiation is handled automatically by pynetdicom.
• Use the DICOM Receiver tab to run a local SCP that accepts incoming files.
help.body_dmwl:
Query a Modality Worklist SCP for scheduled procedures.

─── What is a Modality Worklist? ───────────────────────────
The Modality Worklist (DMWL) service allows a modality (e.g. CT scanner) to
retrieve its scheduled procedure list from the RIS/HIS via the PACS.
This is how scanners auto-populate patient demographics and accession numbers.

─── Station AET field ──────────────────────────────────────
When set, the Station AET value is used as the CALLING AE title for the query.
This impersonates a specific modality's AE title, which is necessary on
systems (e.g. Sectra IDS7, Agfa IMPAX) that filter the worklist by calling AET.
Leave blank to use the local AE configured in Settings.

─── Date field ─────────────────────────────────────────────
Filters by ScheduledProcedureStepStartDate.
Use today's date (YYYYMMDD) to see today's worklist.
Leave blank to return all scheduled items (can be very large).

─── Tips ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
• Double-click a row to view the full SPS sequence with all nested DICOM tags.
• Export to CSV saves the visible columns for reporting or troubleshooting.
• If you get 0 results but expect items, check the Station AET filter.
help.body_commit:
Verify that a remote PACS has permanently and safely stored a set of DICOM instances.

─── How it works ───────────────────────────────────────────
1. Load SOP Instance UIDs from DICOM files on disk.
2. Send N-ACTION — this sends a Storage Commitment Push Model request.
3. The PACS responds asynchronously with N-EVENT-REPORT:
     • Referenced SOP Sequence — successfully committed instances
     • Failed SOP Sequence      — instances the PACS could not commit

─── When to use it ─────────────────────────────────────────
• After C-STORE: confirm the PACS persisted the objects before deleting local copies.
• Audit workflows: verify no data loss after migration or archive restores.
• Troubleshooting: identify which specific instances a PACS claims to have stored.

─── Note ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
Storage Commitment requires the PACS to have an N-EVENT-REPORT callback path back
to this tool. Some PACS systems send it synchronously; others queue it and send
the event minutes later or on a different port.
help.body_iocm:
Send an Instance Availability Notification (N-CREATE) to a remote SCP.

─── What is IOCM? ──────────────────────────────────────────
IOCM (Instance Order Change Management) is used in deletion and
availability-change workflows. When a study is deleted or made unavailable,
an N-CREATE is sent so downstream systems (viewers, archives) can update their
references accordingly.

─── Fields ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Study UID       Instance UID of the study being affected.
  Series UID      Series within that study (optional for study-level events).
  SOP Class UID   DICOM SOP Class of the affected instances.
  SOP Inst UID    Specific instance being reported on.
  Availability    ONLINE | NEARLINE | OFFLINE | UNAVAILABLE

─── Caution ────────────────────────────────────────────────
This operation instructs a PACS that objects have been deleted or are no
longer available. Use carefully — sending incorrect notifications can cause
a PACS to remove valid index entries.
help.body_hl7_send:
Send HL7 v2 messages to a remote MLLP listener.

─── Workflow ───────────────────────────────────────────────
1. Set Host and Port of the destination MLLP server.
2. Select a Template and fill in the patient/procedure fields.
3. Click Load Template — the editor is populated with the filled message.
4. Review or edit the message directly in the editor.
5. Click Send via MLLP.

─── Available Templates ─────────────────────────────────────
  ORM^O01   Radiology order (MSH/PID/PV1/ORC/OBR/ZDS)
  ORU^R01   Radiology report with OBX observation/findings
  ADT^A04   Register new patient (EVN/PID/PV1/PV2)
  ADT^A08   Update existing patient demographics
  ADT^A23   Delete a patient visit
  SIU^S12   Schedule an appointment (SCH/AIS/AIP/AIL)
  SIU^S15   Cancel a scheduled appointment
  QBP^Q22   IHE PDQ patient demographics query
  OML^O21   Lab order with SPM specimen segment

─── MLLP Framing ────────────────────────────────────────────
HL7 v2 over MLLP wraps each message with:
  Start Block:  0x0B  (vertical tab)
  End Block:    0x1C 0x0D  (file separator + carriage return)
Segments are separated by carriage return (\r, 0x0D).

Enable 'Show raw MLLP bytes' to log the full TCP payload including framing.
When log level is set to DEBUG, raw bytes are always captured.
help.body_hl7_recv:
Run a minimal MLLP listener that accepts incoming HL7 v2 messages.

─── What it does ───────────────────────────────────────────
Listens on the specified TCP port for MLLP connections.
For every message received it:
  • Displays the message in the Received Messages box.
  • Sends an automatic AA (Application Accept) ACK back to the sender.
  • Logs the sender's IP address and port.

─── Use cases ──────────────────────────────────────────────
• Test that a RIS/HIS or other HL7 source is sending correctly.
• Capture sample messages for template building or troubleshooting.
• Verify MLLP connectivity and framing before pointing at a real receiver.

─── Notes ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
• Windows Firewall may block the listen port — add an inbound rule if needed.
• Only one listener can run per port. Stop before changing the port.
• Enable 'Show raw MLLP bytes' or set log level to DEBUG to capture framing bytes.
help.body_receiver:
Runs a C-STORE SCP that accepts incoming DICOM objects and saves them to disk.

─── Configuration ───────────────────────────────────────────
  AE Title    What this listener calls itself. Must match the sending device's
              Called AE Title configuration. Default: your local AE from Settings.
  Port        TCP port to listen on. Default: 11112 (standard DICOM port).
  Save to     Directory where received files are written. Created if absent.

─── Accepted SOP Classes ────────────────────────────────────
All standard Storage SOP classes are accepted, including:

  Imaging
    CT  Image Storage              1.2.840.10008.5.1.4.1.1.2
    MR  Image Storage              1.2.840.10008.5.1.4.1.1.4
    PET Image Storage              1.2.840.10008.5.1.4.1.1.128
    NM  Image Storage              1.2.840.10008.5.1.4.1.1.20
    CR  Image Storage              1.2.840.10008.5.1.4.1.1.1
    DX  Image Storage (Pres.)      1.2.840.10008.5.1.4.1.1.1.1
    Digital Mammography (Pres.)    1.2.840.10008.5.1.4.1.1.1.2
    XA  Image Storage              1.2.840.10008.5.1.4.1.1.12.1
    US  Image Storage              1.2.840.10008.5.1.4.1.1.6.1
    Secondary Capture              1.2.840.10008.5.1.4.1.1.7

  Structured Documents
    Basic Text SR                  1.2.840.10008.5.1.4.1.1.88.11
    Enhanced SR                    1.2.840.10008.5.1.4.1.1.88.22
    Comprehensive SR               1.2.840.10008.5.1.4.1.1.88.33
    Comprehensive 3D SR            1.2.840.10008.5.1.4.1.1.88.34
    Key Object Selection           1.2.840.10008.5.1.4.1.1.88.59
    Encapsulated PDF               1.2.840.10008.5.1.4.1.1.104.1

  Presentation States & Plans
    Grayscale Softcopy PS          1.2.840.10008.5.1.4.1.1.11.1
    Color Softcopy PS              1.2.840.10008.5.1.4.1.1.11.2

  … and all other SOP classes registered in pynetdicom's AllStoragePresentationContexts.

─── Accepted Transfer Syntaxes ──────────────────────────────
The listener advertises all of the following transfer syntaxes so it can
accept files regardless of how the sender has compressed them:

  Uncompressed
    Implicit VR Little Endian      1.2.840.10008.1.2
    Explicit VR Little Endian      1.2.840.10008.1.2.1
    Explicit VR Big Endian (ret.)  1.2.840.10008.1.2.2

  JPEG
    JPEG Baseline (Process 1)      1.2.840.10008.1.2.4.50
    JPEG Extended (Process 2 & 4)  1.2.840.10008.1.2.4.51
    JPEG Lossless (Process 14)     1.2.840.10008.1.2.4.57
    JPEG Lossless SV1              1.2.840.10008.1.2.4.70

  JPEG-LS
    JPEG-LS Lossless               1.2.840.10008.1.2.4.80
    JPEG-LS Near-Lossless          1.2.840.10008.1.2.4.81

  JPEG 2000
    JPEG 2000 Lossless             1.2.840.10008.1.2.4.90
    JPEG 2000 Lossy                1.2.840.10008.1.2.4.91
    JPEG 2000 MC Lossless          1.2.840.10008.1.2.4.92
    JPEG 2000 MC Lossy             1.2.840.10008.1.2.4.93

  Other
    RLE Lossless                   1.2.840.10008.1.2.5

─── Storage layout ──────────────────────────────────────────
Received files are organised into a two-level hierarchy:

  <save_dir>/<StudyInstanceUID>/<SeriesInstanceUID>/<SOPInstanceUID>.dcm

This keeps every series together so the built-in stack viewer can scroll
through CT/MR stacks in correct InstanceNumber order.

─── Viewing received studies ────────────────────────────────
The Received Studies card lists all studies and series. Click "View ▶" on
any series to open the embedded dwv DICOM viewer with:
  • Proper Hounsfield windowing (Window Centre / Width)
  • Scroll through the stack with mousewheel or the Scroll tool
  • Zoom / Pan and Window-Level adjustment

─── Tips ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
• Make sure no other process is already using the configured port.
• The sending device must have this tool's AE Title in its routing table.
• Restart the listener (Stop → Start) after changing port or save directory.
• Use the C-STORE tab to test-send a file and confirm round-trip storage.
help.body_settings:
Configure the local AE, remote AE presets, HL7 defaults, and general options.

─── Roles & Access ──────────────────────────────────────────────
PACS Admin Tool has two user roles:

  admin   Full access. Can manage users, change any user's password,
          and modify all system-wide configuration.

  user    Standard access. Can use all DICOM tools and tabs, manage
          personal preferences, and change their own password.

Admin-only capabilities:
  • User Management — create, view, and delete user accounts.
  • Change any user's password (users can only change their own).
  • All system-wide configuration:
    Local AE, Remote AE Presets, DICOMweb Presets (System),
    HL7, Web Server, Logging, Telemetry.
    Regular users see these sections as read-only (🔒 banner shown).

Regular users can:
  • Use every DICOM/HL7 tab (C-FIND, C-STORE, Receiver, Inspector, etc.).
  • Manage their own private presets (My DICOM Presets, My DICOMweb Presets).
  • Toggle advanced tabs and change their personal preferences.
  • Change their own password.

─── My Preferences (per-user) ──────────────────────────────────
Settings in this section are saved per-user and do not affect other users.

• Show advanced tabs — reveals five specialist tabs hidden by default:
  Storage Commit, IOCM, KOS Creator, UID Remapper, and DICOMweb.
  You can also toggle this instantly with the '▸ Advanced' button
  at the right end of the tab bar.

• My DICOM Presets — private AE presets visible only to you.
  System presets (shared with all users) are managed in the
  'Saved Remote AE Presets' section above.
  Both appear in every AE preset dropdown, grouped separately.

• My DICOMweb Presets — private DICOMweb server presets (URL + auth)
  visible only to you. System DICOMweb presets are managed via the
  DICOMweb tab's Save Preset button.

─── System settings (shared) ────────────────────────────────────
Changes to Local AE, Remote AE Presets, HL7, Web Server, Logging,
and Telemetry are shared across all users of this instance.
Only admins can save changes to system settings.
help.body_sr_viewer:
Parse and display DICOM Structured Report (SR) files as an interactive tree.

─── What is a DICOM SR? ─────────────────────────────────────
A Structured Report is a DICOM object that encodes clinical findings,
measurements, and observations in a standardised hierarchical structure.
Common examples: radiology reports, radiation dose reports, CAD results.

─── Workflow ───────────────────────────────────────────────
1. Click Browse — select a .dcm file that contains a Structured Report.
2. Click Parse SR to parse and render the report tree.
3. Optionally click View Raw DICOM Tags to inspect all DICOM attributes.

─── Tree Viewer ─────────────────────────────────────────────
• CONTAINER nodes render as collapsible sections with a ▼/▶ toggle.
• Each item shows a colour-coded type badge (CONTAINER, NUM, CODE, TEXT …)
  and a relationship badge (CONTAINS, HAS OBS CONTEXT …).
• Concept names coded in the DCM scheme are clickable links to the
  DICOM PS3.16 standard (NEMA website, opens in a new tab).
• Use the filter bar to search across concept names and values.
  Results are shown as a flat table when a filter is active.
• Expand all / Collapse all buttons control the entire tree at once.

─── Supported SR SOP Classes ───────────────────────────────
Basic Text SR · Enhanced SR · Comprehensive SR · Comprehensive 3D SR
X-Ray Radiation Dose SR · Patient Radiation Dose SR · Enhanced X-Ray Dose SR
Mammography CAD SR · Chest CAD SR · Colon CAD SR
Simplified Adult Echo SR · Acquisition Context SR · and others.

─── Supported content item types ────────────────────────────
CONTAINER  NUM (with units)  TEXT  CODE  IMAGE  UIDREF
PNAME  DATE  TIME  DATETIME  SCOORD  SCOORD3D  TCOORD  COMPOSITE  WAVEFORM

─── Tips ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
• Click any CONTAINER header to collapse/expand its children.
• Click a DCM-linked concept name to look it up in the NEMA standard.
• Use View Raw DICOM Tags for low-level debugging of the SR dataset.
help.body_kos_creator:
Create a DICOM Key Object Selection (KOS) document referencing a set of instances.

─── What is a KOS? ─────────────────────────────────────────
A Key Object Selection document is a DICOM SR object that acts as a manifest,
pointing to a chosen subset of images or other instances within a study.
Use cases: teaching files, relevant priors, XDS-I manifests, surgery planning sets.

─── Workflow ───────────────────────────────────────────────
1. Click Browse to load one or more DICOM files from the study.
2. Click Extract Metadata to auto-fill the Study & Patient fields.
3. Review or edit the Study fields and the Referenced Instances list.
4. Choose a Document Title from the dropdown.
5. Click Create KOS & Save to write the KOS to a .dcm file, or
   Create KOS & Send (C-STORE) to store it directly on a PACS.

─── Referenced Instances format ────────────────────────────
Each line in the instances box must follow this format:
  SeriesUID | SOPClassUID | SOPInstanceUID
Lines starting with # are treated as comments and ignored.

─── Document Title codes ───────────────────────────────────
  Of Interest           General key image selection.
  Best In Set           Best single representative image.
  For Referring         Selected images for the referring physician.
  For Surgery           Pre-operative image selection.
  For Teaching          Teaching file selection.
  For Conference        Conference presentation selection.
  For Therapy           Therapy planning image selection.
  For Research          Research or trial image selection.
  XDS-I Manifest        IHE Cross-Enterprise Document Sharing manifest.
help.body_dicomize:
Convert non-DICOM files (PDF, images, video) into valid DICOM objects.

─── Shared Patient & Study Data ─────────────────────────────
All three sub-tabs share a collapsible patient/study data card at the top.
Fill in the fields once; they apply to every conversion in the current session.
Click the header or the ▼ arrow to hide the card after filling it in.
• Study Instance UID — enter one to link conversions to an existing study,
  or click Generate to create a fresh RFC 4122-based UID (2.25.…).

─── Fill from Worklist ──────────────────────────────────────
Click "Fill from Worklist…" in the Patient & Study header to query a DICOM
Modality Worklist and auto-populate all patient and study fields from a
selected worklist item.
This replicates how a real modality works: it queries the DMWL, then the
PACS automatically attaches received images to the correct study.

─── PDF to DICOM ────────────────────────────────────────────
Wraps the PDF as-is inside an Encapsulated PDF Storage object.
SOP Class  1.2.840.10008.5.1.4.1.1.104.1  (Encapsulated PDF Storage)
Modality   DOC
• The original PDF bytes are stored verbatim — no rendering or re-encoding.
• The optional Document Title field maps to the DICOM DocumentTitle tag.

─── Images to DICOM ─────────────────────────────────────────
Converts raster images (JPEG, PNG, BMP, TIFF, WebP, JFIF …) to
Secondary Capture DICOM files.
SOP Class  1.2.840.10008.5.1.4.1.1.7  (Secondary Capture Image Storage)
Modality   OT
• Images are converted to 8-bit RGB before encoding (no lossy recompression).
• Select multiple files: each becomes a separate .dcm in the same series.
  Multiple results are delivered as a single ZIP archive.

─── Video to DICOM ──────────────────────────────────────────
Two output formats are available:

  Encapsulated Video (default)
    SOP Class  1.2.840.10008.5.1.4.1.1.77.1.2.1  (Video Photographic Image Storage)
    Transfer   1.2.840.10008.1.2.4.102  (MPEG-4 AVC/H.264 HP)
    Modality   XC
    The video bitstream is stored verbatim — no transcoding.
    Dimensions and frame count are parsed from the MP4/MOV file header.
    Note: some DICOM viewers open this in an external video player.

  Multi-frame JPEG
    SOP Class  1.2.840.10008.5.1.4.1.1.7.4  (Multi-frame True Color Secondary Capture)
    Transfer   1.2.840.10008.1.2.4.50  (JPEG Baseline)
    Modality   OT
    Frames are extracted using ffmpeg at up to 10 fps and stored as individual
    JPEG-compressed frames in the pixel data. Plays as a native cine loop in
    all standard DICOM viewers without needing an external video player.
    Requires: ffmpeg installed on the server running PACSAdminTool.

─── Download vs Send to PACS ────────────────────────────────
Download DICOM  Converts immediately and triggers a browser download.
                Returns a single .dcm or a .zip (multiple images).

Send to PACS    Converts then C-STOREs to the specified remote AE.
                Click the button to reveal the AE selector;
                choose a preset or enter host/port/AE title manually.
                The log shows per-file success or failure.
help.body_cget:
Both C-MOVE and C-GET retrieve DICOM objects from a remote PACS, but they work very differently.

─── C-MOVE ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
The SCU (this tool) sends a C-MOVE request identifying which study to retrieve
and names a THIRD AE as the destination.
The source PACS then opens a NEW outbound connection to that destination AE
and pushes the files as C-STORE operations.

  Flow:  This Tool → (C-MOVE req) → Source PACS
                                        ↓
                             Destination SCP ← (C-STORE)

  Requirements:
  • The destination AE must have a running Storage SCP.
  • The destination AE Title must be configured on the source PACS
    (i.e. the PACS must know its host and port).
  • A firewall port must be open INBOUND on the destination host.

  Use C-MOVE when:
  • Sending images to another PACS or archive.
  • The destination is a well-known, network-accessible system.
  • You do not need the files on this machine.

─── C-GET ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
The SCU (this tool) sends a C-GET request and the source PACS responds by
pushing files back WITHIN THE SAME ASSOCIATION using C-STORE sub-operations.
No third-party AE or inbound firewall port is required.

  Flow:  This Tool → (C-GET req) → Source PACS
         This Tool ← (C-STORE)   ←

  Requirements:
  • The source PACS must support the C-GET service (optional per standard).
  • No additional destination AE registration is needed.
  • Files are saved directly to the directory you specify.

  Use C-GET when:
  • You want files on the machine running this tool.
  • Firewall rules prevent the source PACS from opening inbound connections
    to a destination (a common situation in clinical networks).
  • The source PACS supports C-GET (check vendor docs — not all do).

─── Choosing ────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Scenario                                       Recommended
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Move to another PACS/archive                   C-MOVE
  Pull to this workstation, no inbound port       C-GET
  Source PACS does not support C-GET             C-MOVE
  Both AEs on same subnet, no firewall issues    Either

─── Selecting in the C-FIND tab ────────────────────────────
1. Tick the checkbox(es) next to the studies you want to retrieve.
2. In the Retrieve card, choose C-MOVE or C-GET.
   • C-MOVE: enter the AE Title of the destination SCP.
   • C-GET:  enter the local directory to save files into.
3. Click the retrieve button.
   Multiple checked studies are submitted sequentially.
help.body_inspector:
Inspect, edit, and compare DICOM tags without a PACS connection.

─── Inspect & Edit sub-tab ────────────────────────────────────
1. Click Browse and select any DICOM file.
2. Click Parse & Inspect.
3. A metadata summary appears (Patient, Modality, SOPClass, etc.).
4. Click View All DICOM Tags to open the full filterable tag tree.
5. Click Edit Tags… to open the inline Tag Editor:
   • Edit any tag value in-place and track pending changes.
   • Download Modified File — saves the edited DICOM to your computer.
   • Send to PACS — C-STOREs the edited file to any configured AE.

─── Diff sub-tab ───────────────────────────────────────────────
Compare two DICOM files tag-by-tag:
1. Select File A and File B.
2. Click Compare.
3. The results table shows Changed / Only in A / Only in B / Identical rows.
   Toggle 'Show differences only' to hide identical tags.

─── Use cases ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
• Fix wrong tags (patient name, accession number) before re-ingestion.
• Verify an anonymisation or correction was applied correctly.
• Compare the same study from two different systems.
• Debug Transfer Syntax or SOP class mismatches.
help.body_anonymizer:
Strip or replace Protected Health Information (PHI) from DICOM files.

─── Profiles ───────────────────────────────────────────────
  Basic  Replaces PatientName and PatientID, removes birth date, sex, age,
         size/weight, institution name/address, referring/performing physicians,
         operator names, accession number, and other patient identifiers.

  Full   Everything in Basic plus study and series descriptions, protocol name,
         and scheduled procedure step fields.

─── Workflow ───────────────────────────────────────────────
1. Click Add Files and select one or more .dcm files.
2. Choose a profile and set the replacement patient name and ID.
3. Choose an output action:

   Anonymise & Download ZIP
     Converts the file(s) and triggers a browser download of a ZIP archive.
     Original files are never modified.

   Anonymise & Send (C-STORE)
     Anonymises the file(s) then immediately C-STOREs them to a remote PACS.
     Select a preset or enter host/port/AE title, then click the button.
     The status line shows success or failure per file.

─── Important notes ────────────────────────────────────────
• UIDs (StudyInstanceUID, SeriesInstanceUID, SOPInstanceUID) are NOT
  altered by default. If de-identification of UIDs is required for your
  use case, process the files further with a dedicated de-identification tool.
• Burned-in PHI in pixel data (e.g. scanned documents, some ultrasound images)
  is NOT removed by this tool.
• For clinical de-identification workflows, always validate the result and
  follow your institution's data governance policy.
help.body_dicomdir:
Parse and browse, or generate, a DICOMDIR index file.

─── What is DICOMDIR? ──────────────────────────────────────
A DICOMDIR file is an index that maps Patient / Study / Series / Instance
hierarchies to the actual DICOM files stored in the same media root.
It allows viewers and systems to navigate disc contents without reading
every individual file.

─── Reading a DICOMDIR ─────────────────────────────────────
1. Click Browse and select the DICOMDIR file (no extension, or as provided).
2. Click Parse DICOMDIR.
3. A collapsible tree is displayed:
     👤 Patient   [PatientID]
       📋 Study   [Date · Description · AccessionNumber]
         🔷 Series [Modality · Number · Description]
           Instance [Type · InstanceNumber · SOPInstanceUID]

Tips:
• The referenced file paths shown are relative to the media root (CD/DVD root).
• Use this to quickly audit disc contents before importing into a PACS.
• Supports non-standard DICOMDIR files that omit PATIENT-level records.

─── Generating a DICOMDIR ──────────────────────────────────
Build a new DICOMDIR index from a set of DICOM files.

1. Click Select Files… to pick individual .dcm files, or
   Click Select Folder… to recursively include all DICOM files in a directory.
2. Click Generate & Download ZIP.
   A ZIP archive is downloaded containing:
     DICOMDIR  — the generated index file
     DICOM/    — all selected instances organised by Study/Series/SOP UID

Use cases:
• Create a patient disc (CD/DVD) from a local DICOM collection.
• Package a study for transport or archive in the standard media format.
• Re-index a folder of DICOM files that originally had no DICOMDIR.
help.body_dashboard:
The Dashboard gives you a quick overview of the tool's current state.

─── Services ───────────────────────────────────────────────
Shows whether the DICOM Storage SCP and the HL7 listener are currently running,
and under which AE title the SCP is registered.

─── AE Connectivity ────────────────────────────────────────
Lists all remote AE presets saved in Settings.
Click Test All AEs to run a C-ECHO ping against each preset simultaneously.
Results appear live as each ping completes:
  ● Green OK    – C-ECHO succeeded (remote AE is reachable and responding).
  ● Red Fail    – association rejected, refused, or timed out.

─── Recent Activity ────────────────────────────────────────
Shows the last 20 entries from the audit log — who did what and when.
Click Refresh to reload.
For the full log with filtering, use the Logs tab.
help.body_logs:
View the application log in real time.

─── Controls ───────────────────────────────────────────────
  Log File      Select which log file to view:
                  pacs_admin.log — main application log (web server)
                Logs rotate daily; up to 7 days of history are kept.

  Last N lines  Number of lines from the end of the file to load.
                Increase for deeper history; decrease for faster loads.

  Level filter  Restrict display to a specific severity:
                  DEBUG    Verbose networking detail (pynetdicom PDUs, etc.)
                  INFO     Normal operational messages.
                  WARNING  Non-fatal issues worth investigating.
                  ERROR    Errors that prevented an operation from completing.
                All Levels shows everything.

  Substring filter  Free-text search across all visible log lines.

─── Tips ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
• Set the log level to DEBUG in Settings to capture full DICOM network traces,
  including every PDU sent and received during C-FIND, C-STORE, etc.
• Use the Substring filter to find a specific AE title, patient, or error.
• Log files are stored in the logs/ folder inside the application data
  directory shown in the About tab.
help.body_about:
The About tab shows the running version, checks for updates, and lists credits.

─── Checking for Updates ────────────────────────────────────
Click "Check for updates" to compare the running version against the latest
GitHub release. If a newer version is available, an update card appears with
release notes and action buttons.

─── Updating — by distribution type ────────────────────────

  Docker / Container
  ───────────────────
  Run the following commands to pull the new image and restart:
    docker compose pull
    docker compose up -d
  The container is replaced automatically; no data is lost.

  Windows Executable (.exe)
  ──────────────────────────
  Click "Download Update" to open the GitHub releases page.
  Download PacsAdminTool.exe or PacsAdminToolWeb.exe and replace the
  existing executable. No installer needed.

  Install & Restart (source / self-updating builds)
  ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Click "Install & Restart". The update is downloaded and applied;
  the server restarts automatically and the browser page reloads.

─── Data folder ─────────────────────────────────────────────
The path shown in the About section is the application data folder where
configuration, logs, and the audit trail are stored.
Click the path to copy it to the clipboard.

─── Credits ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
Source code and bundled library licences are listed in the About tab.
This application was built with the assistance of Claude (Anthropic).
help.body_dicomweb:
DICOM PS3.18 RESTful web services — an HTTP-based alternative to C-FIND / C-STORE / C-MOVE.

─── Connecting to your PACS ─────────────────────────────────────
Enter the DICOMweb base URL for your PACS server (e.g. Sectra IDS7, Agfa IMPAX,
Orthanc, dcm4chee, Horos, etc.) and select the appropriate authentication type.
Click 'Test Connection' to verify reachability before running queries.
Save the configuration as a named preset so you don't have to re-enter it.

For Sectra IDS7: check the Sectra admin console under Web Services for the
DICOMweb base URL (typically https://<host>/dicomweb or https://<host>/IDS7/dicomweb).
Use Basic Auth with a service account, or Bearer Token if your site uses OAuth.

─── Authentication types ────────────────────────────────────────
• None — plain HTTP/HTTPS, no credentials (e.g. local Orthanc dev instance)
• Basic — HTTP Basic Auth: username + password (most common)
• Bearer Token — OAuth 2.0 / API key in the Authorization header

─── Sub-tabs ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
• QIDO-RS — Query studies, series, or instances by DICOM attributes
• STOW-RS — Upload .dcm files to the server (multipart POST)
• WADO-RS — Retrieve instances; response packaged as a downloadable ZIP

─── Presets ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Save frequently-used server configurations as named presets.
System presets are shared with all users; My Presets are personal.
This tab is only visible when 'Show advanced tabs' is enabled in
My Preferences (Settings tab) or via the '▸ Advanced' button.
help.body_validator:
Validate a DICOM file against the DICOM standard and identify conformance issues.

─── What it checks ──────────────────────────────────────────
File Meta Information (Group 0002)
  • Transfer Syntax UID present and valid format
  • MediaStorageSOPClassUID and MediaStorageSOPInstanceUID present
  • Cross-check between File Meta and dataset-level UIDs

Core Identifiers
  • SOPClassUID (0008,0016) — Type 1 required
  • SOPInstanceUID (0008,0018) — Type 1 required
  • StudyInstanceUID (0020,000D) — Type 1 required
  • SeriesInstanceUID (0020,000E) — Type 1 required
  • Modality (0008,0060) — Type 1 required
  • All UIDs validated against DICOM UID format rules
  • UID uniqueness (Study/Series/SOP collision detection)
  • Retired SOP Class detection

Patient Module
  • PatientName (0010,0010) — Type 2
  • PatientID (0010,0020) — Type 2
  • PatientBirthDate, PatientSex — Type 2

General Study Module
  • StudyDate (0008,0020) — Type 2
  • AccessionNumber (0008,0050) — Type 2
  • ReferringPhysicianName (0008,0090) — Type 2

Pixel Data (image SOP classes only)
  • Pixel Data presence consistent with BitsAllocated
  • BitsStored must not exceed BitsAllocated
  • HighBit = BitsStored - 1 check
  • Rows and Columns present
  • PixelSpacing present for CT/MR/PET/NM
  • PhotometricInterpretation present

General
  • SpecificCharacterSet — recommended for non-ASCII names
  • Private tag inventory (informational)

─── Severity levels ─────────────────────────────────────────
  Error    The file violates a MUST requirement. Most PACS and viewer
           software will reject or mishandle this file.
  Warning  The file violates a SHOULD requirement or contains an
           unusual value. File will likely work but may cause issues
           on some systems.
  Info     Advisory finding — file is valid but something is worth
           noting (private tags, missing Type 2 optional tags, etc.).

─── Tips ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
• A clean validation (no errors) does not guarantee every PACS will
  accept the file — vendor-specific requirements may apply.
• For images with burned-in PHI, use the Anonymizer tab after validation.
• Use the DICOM Inspector tab to examine all tags in detail.
help.body_uid_remap:
Generate fresh Study / Series / Instance UIDs for a batch of DICOM files.

─── Why you need this ──────────────────────────────────────────
Most PACS systems silently reject a C-STORE when the StudyInstanceUID already
exists in their database. The UID Remapper solves this by creating brand-new
UIDs while preserving all other tag content and internal referential integrity
(all files in the same original study get the same new StudyInstanceUID, etc.).

─── Workflow ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
1. Select one or more DICOM files (all from the same study/series if
   you want consistent UID re-mapping across files).
2. Choose the Remap level:
   • Study only — only StudyInstanceUID is replaced.
   • Study + Series — Study + SeriesInstanceUID.
   • Study + Series + Instance — all three UIDs replaced (safest option).
3. Optionally set a custom UID prefix (default: 2.25. — UUID-based root).
4. Click Preview to see the old → new UID mapping before committing.
5. Click Download Remapped ZIP to get the modified DICOM files.
6. C-STORE the remapped files using the C-STORE tab or DICOMweb STOW-RS.

─── Notes ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
• The UID prefix '2.25.' followed by a UUID integer is a DICOM-conformant
  way to generate globally unique UIDs without a registered root.
• MediaStorageSOPInstanceUID in the file meta group is also updated when
  remapping at instance level.

about.title: PACS Admin Tool
about.description:
A portable DICOM & HL7 administration utility for PACS engineers,
radiologists, and healthcare IT professionals.
about.description_html:
A portable DICOM & HL7 administration utility for PACS engineers,
radiologists, and healthcare IT professionals.
about.supports:
Supports: C-ECHO · C-FIND · C-STORE · C-MOVE · DMWL · Storage Commitment
          IOCM · HL7 v2 (MLLP) Send & Receive · DICOM Storage SCP
about.supports_html:
Supports: C-ECHO · C-FIND · C-STORE · C-MOVE · DMWL · Storage Commitment
          IOCM · HL7 v2 (MLLP) Send & Receive · DICOM Storage SCP
about.claude_credit:
This entire application was designed and built with the assistance of Claude,
Anthropic's AI assistant — from the DICOM/HL7 protocol logic to the UI.
about.claude_credit_html:
This entire application was designed and built with the assistance of Claude,
Anthropic's AI assistant — from the DICOM/HL7 protocol logic to the UI.
about.tech_stack: Built with Python · pynetdicom · pydicom · hl7 · Tkinter · Flask · Flask-SocketIO
about.tech_stack_web: Built with Python · pynetdicom · pydicom · hl7 · Flask · Flask-SocketIO

sr_viewer.select_file: Select a DICOM Structured Report file
sr_viewer.no_file: No file selected. Use Browse to pick a DICOM SR file.
sr_viewer.no_parsed: No parsed SR available. Parse a file first.
sr_viewer.about_what_is_body: A DICOM Structured Report (SR) encodes clinical information in a machine-readable, hierarchical tree of coded concepts. Unlike a PDF scan or plain text, SR content can be queried, extracted, and validated by software — enabling dose monitoring, CAD integration, measurement tracking, and decision support workflows.
sr_viewer.about_root_concept: The document title (shown in the meta bar after parsing) comes from the root (0040,A043) ConceptNameCodeSequence and identifies what kind of SR you are looking at — for example Diagnostic imaging report (LN:81220-6) for a radiology report, or X-Ray Radiation Dose Report (DCM:113701) for a dose SR.
sr_viewer.about_use_enhanced: Reports with coded content mixed with free text
sr_viewer.about_use_rdsr: Dose monitoring (RDSR); generated by CT, fluoro and cath-lab equipment
sr_viewer.about_use_rdsr_enh: Detailed procedural dose; successor to RDSR for newer equipment
sr_viewer.about_use_prdsr: Cumulative patient-level dose tracking across procedures
sr_viewer.about_use_echo: Structured cardiac echo measurements and findings
sr_viewer.about_use_kos: Manifest of key images within a study; used for image sharing (XDS-I)
sr_viewer.about_scheme_dcm: DICOM-specific concepts: dose, modalities, finding codes. Clicking a DCM-coded concept in the tree opens the NEMA reference.
sr_viewer.about_scheme_ln: Report document types and clinical observations. Common: LN:81220-6 = Diagnostic imaging report.
sr_viewer.about_scheme_ucum: Measurement units in NUM items: mGy, mm², %, mSv, etc.
sr_viewer.about_vtype_container: Groups related items; collapsible in the tree

kos_creator.select_files: Select DICOM files to extract metadata from
kos_creator.no_files_loaded_msg: No DICOM files loaded. Use Browse to select files first.
kos_creator.instances_hint: Format per line:  SeriesUID | SOPClassUID | SOPInstanceUID  (lines starting with # are ignored)

telemetry.consent_body: PACS Admin Tool collects anonymous usage data (app version, deployment type, OS, language, and which features are used). No patient data, AE titles, hostnames, or IP addresses are collected. Data is sent to PostHog on EU servers. You can opt out at any time in Settings.
telemetry.settings_note: Collects: app version, deployment type (Docker / exe / source), OS, interface language, and which features are used. No patient data, AE titles, hostnames, or IP addresses. Data is sent to PostHog on EU servers. Changes take effect immediately.

tag_editor.title: Tag Editor
tag_editor.no_file: No file loaded in the Inspector.
tag_editor.fill_ae_fields: Fill in AE Title, Host, and a valid Port before sending.

dicomize.image_accept: JPEG, PNG, BMP, TIFF, WebP, JFIF and other raster formats
dicomize.image_note: Each image becomes a separate Secondary Capture DICOM file. Multiple files are returned as a ZIP archive.
dicomize.video_accept: MP4, MOV and other video formats
dicomize.video_note: The video is stored as-is using DICOM Encapsulated Video (MPEG-4 AVC/H.264 transfer syntax). Dimensions and frame count are read from the file header.
dicomize.wl_select_hint: Click a row to fill Patient & Study data in the DICOMize form.
dicomize.wl_empty: Query a worklist to see items here.
dicomize.wl_need_ae: Enter AE host, port, and title first.
dicomize.video_format_enc_note: — stores MP4/MOV as-is, small file, MPEG-4 transfer syntax
dicomize.video_format_mf_note: — extracts frames, native playback in all viewers, requires ffmpeg on server
dicomize.mixed_note: Select any combination of images, PDFs, and videos. Each file is auto-detected and converted to the appropriate DICOM format.
dicomize.preview_unsupported: Preview not available for this file type.
dicomize.oruian_title: Send ORU IAN to EMR (pre-order workflow)
dicomize.oruian_desc: Send an ORU^R01 Image Availability Notification to your EMR. The EMR creates an examination ad-hoc and returns an ORM. When the ORM arrives via the HL7 listener, click Fill from ORM to auto-populate the patient & study fields — then send the images to PACS.
dicomize.oruian_editor_placeholder: Load a template or type your HL7 message here…
dicomize.oruian_no_msg: No HL7 message body — load a template first.
dicomize.oruian_no_host: Enter EMR host and port first.
dicomize.oruian_ack_ok: ACK received. Waiting for ORM from EMR…
dicomize.oruian_orm_received: ORM received! Click "Fill from ORM" to populate patient & study fields.
dicomize.group_series: Group into one series (shared Series UID)
dicomize.meta_preview_hint: Shown before every download/store. Updates automatically when you change the form.
dicomize.orm_map_desc: Map HL7 segment fields (e.g. OBR.3) to DICOM metadata. Changes are saved to configuration.
dicomize.duplicate_warning: Study UID already exists in PACS ({count} instance(s)). Send anyway?

validator.verdict_errors: ✗  {n} error(s) and {w} warning(s) found. File may not be accepted by all PACS systems.
